paper_title: Chronological Framework for Mongolia's Early Pastoral Cultures

methods.description: This method involves the collection and preparation of human bone and teeth samples for radiocarbon dating to establish a chronological framework for Mongolia's early pastoral cultures.

steps.description: Sampling was conducted at the National University of Mongolia on human tooth and bone.
steps.searchable_summary: collect human tooth and bone samples for analysis

steps.description: For samples of bone, pieces weighing between 0.5 and 2 g were collected and labeled in individual plastic bags.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare bone samples by weighing and labeling

steps.description: Whole teeth were sent intact for analysis, and when teeth had preexisting fractures, dentine samples of ~200 mg were drilled with a Dremel 3000 tool and collected into Eppendorf tubes within a clean laboratory environment.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare tooth samples by drilling and collecting dentine

steps.description: When drilling was necessary, all associated laboratory surfaces and Dremel parts were cleaned with methanol between each sample.
steps.searchable_summary: clean laboratory surfaces and tools with methanol

steps.description: Samples were decalcified over at least a 24-hour period using mild acid (HCl, 2-4% w/vol; RT) at the Center for Stable Isotope Research at the University of Groningen.
Soft and pliable fragments were rinsed thoroughly with demineralised water.
steps.searchable_summary: rinse decalcified fragments with demineralised water

steps.description: Extracts were then exposed to NaOH (1%, ~30 min) to eliminate humic acids, rinsed to neutrality and treated once more with acid (HCl, 4% w/vol, 15 min).
steps.searchable_summary: treat extracts with NaOH and HCl to remove humic acids

steps.description: The raw collagen fraction was denatured to gelatin in acidified demineralised water (pH 3) at 80˚C for 18 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: denature collagen to gelatin in acidified water

steps.description: The dissolved gelatin was filtered through a 50 μm mesh to eliminate any remaining foreign particulates.
Approximately 4 mg aliquots of the reduced carbon fraction were then weighed into tin capsules for combustion in an Elemental Analyser.
steps.searchable_summary: weigh carbon fraction into tin capsules for combustion

steps.description: The EA was coupled to an Isotope Ratio Mass Spectrometer (IRMS), allowing the d 13 C value of the sample to be measured.
steps.searchable_summary: measure d 13 C value using IRMS

steps.description: The CO 2 gas reduced to graphite over an Fe(s) catalyst.
steps.searchable_summary: reduce CO 2 to graphite using Fe catalyst

steps.description: The graphite samples were then pressed, and the radioisotopic ratio determined on a MICADAS accelerator mass spectrometer.

methods.description: This method involves creating a trait-based typological classification to define cultural units and modeling burial traditions using Bayesian methods to understand the chronological sequence of early pastoral cultures.

steps.description: Produced a trait-based typological classification using specific features of construction of burial and monument features to create cultural units.
steps.searchable_summary: classify cultural units based on burial and monument features

steps.description: Modeled burials according to both trait-based types and body position within the burials using a Bayesian uniform phase model in the program OxCal.
steps.searchable_summary: model burial traditions using Bayesian phase model in OxCal

steps.description: Repeated analyses using a trapezoidal prior probability distribution and a simple outlier model to evaluate model influence by outliers.
steps.searchable_summary: validate model using trapezoidal distribution and outlier model

steps.description: Performed further tests of relative ordering using the 'Order' function in OxCal.
steps.searchable_summary: test relative ordering using OxCal 'Order' function
